articles.title: Sagittarius, Weekly Horoscope, June 21 to June 26, 2026: Caution is advised before commitments
articles.description: Sagittarius, this week brings a refreshing shift with improved work conditions and easing anxiety. Expect helpful encounters early on, while family health shows mixed but hopeful signs. Midweek offers growing respect and a wish fulfilled, though caution is advised before commitments. The weekend brings unexpected guests and financial strain, urging restraint and self-care. Love blossoms with deeper connection, but Saturday demands careful handling of domestic peace.

articles.title: Norway Bans AI in Elementary Schools
articles.description: Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre argued that using AI could increase the risk that young children miss important steps in their education.

articles.title: Nasa’s daring mission to save a space telescope from falling back to Earth
articles.description: After more than 20 years of groundbreaking discoveries, Nasa’s Swift space observatory is losing altitude and heading towards Earth. A bold rescue mission could give the telescope a new lease on life.

articles.title: Jio Call Agent Explained: How It Will Work On Your Phone
articles.description: Reliance Jio has launched an innovative Call Agent feature aimed at enhancing user experience during calls. Activated by saying "Hey Jio," it allows for real-time transcription, task management, and summarization in multiple Indian languages, all while prioritizing user consent.

articles.title: ‘Hackers’ send fake alert to thousands of phones in Brazil: What the officials said
articles.description: Brazil's emergency alert system was compromised on Saturday, sending thousands of citizens fake messages containing the word "misanthropy." The government suspects a cyberattack after the system was remotely triggered and subsequently taken offline. Officials are working with the Federal Police to restore the platform securely, emphasizing the breach originated from outside the national civil defense network.

articles.title: VLC creator turns attention to robots, raises $5 million for real-time device platform
articles.description: Jean-Baptiste Kempf, the lead developer behind VLC Media Player, is betting that millions of robots and drones will soon need the same kind of low-latency technology that helped make video streaming seamless.

articles.title: Granta stops publishing short story award winners over AI controversy
articles.description: Literary magazine will no longer engage in ‘external publishing partnerships’ after Commonwealth prize furore The prominent literary magazine Granta will no longer publish the winning entries of the annual Commonwealth short story prize after one of this year’s winners drew widespread accusations of AI use. The magazine said it would no longer be involved in “external publishing partnerships” in which it had no editorial control. Continue reading...

articles.title: What If You Want To Repair Or Sell Your EV?
articles.description: Delhi's bustling used car market faces a seismic shift as electric vehicles gain traction. While mechanics struggle with EV repairs, citing complex systems and lack of training, the city's push for cleaner mobility is evident. Dealers grapple with battery concerns and resale challenges, as the automotive ecosystem slowly adapts to the electric future, leaving many traditional players uncertain about their place.

articles.title: In 1926, a secretary noticed short lines between columns and highlighted the growing need for fax technology
articles.description: Long before fax machines became office staples, the concept of transmitting documents remotely was evolving. From Alexander Bain's 1843 patent to early 20th-century newspaper photo transmissions, the journey was driven by practical office needs. This evolution, often overlooked, highlights how everyday workers identified inefficiencies, paving the way for revolutionary communication tools that transformed business.

articles.description: ET Most Innovative AI Product Awards 2026 acknowledges AI products that are altering the medical field with faster, smarter and improved diagnostics, clinical decisions and patient outcomes. The Healthcare & MedTech category gives recognition to AI-powered products, digital health platforms and enterprise AI solutions delivering measurable impact at scale, as AI innovation in India heats up.

articles.title: A City on the Move: How Mumbai Is Handling Millions Through Better Connectivity | News18
articles.description: Mumbai is undergoing a transformative infrastructure revolution aimed at meeting the needs of one of the world's most densely populated urban centers. With a network of new metro corridors, coastal roads, sea links, upgraded rail systems, and modern highways, the city is significantly improving connectivity and reducing travel times for millions of daily commuters.These mega infrastructure projects are reshaping urban mobility by integrating multiple modes of transport into a seamless network. Enhanced public transportation, expanded road capacity, and smarter traffic management systems are helping Mumbai move people more efficiently while supporting economic growth and improving quality of life.As India's financial capital continues to expand, investments in world-class infrastructure are ensuring that Mumbai remains resilient, connected, and future-ready. By creating faster, safer, and more sustainable transport options, the city is building the foundation for a modern metropolis capable of handling the demands of a growing population while driving development for decades to come. n18oc_breaking-newsn18oc_IndiaNews18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.description: COAI, India's digital communications body, held its annual meeting, electing Rahul Vatts of Bharti Airtel as Chairperson and Dr. Ravi P. Gandhi of Reliance Jio as Vice-Chairperson for 2026-27. The organization emphasized the sector's crucial role in India's digital economy, highlighting the evolution of technologies like 5G Advanced and AI. COAI pledged to collaborate with stakeholders for trusted, secure, and future-ready infrastructure to boost economic growth.

articles.title: Hack of the Day: How to build a smart home on a budget of under Rs 10,000
articles.description: Transform your home into a smart haven without breaking the bank. For under ₹10,000, you can achieve voice-controlled lights, automated appliances, and basic security by building your setup in phases. Start with an affordable voice hub, then add smart plugs and bulbs. Finally, integrate security cameras and sensors for a truly connected and convenient living experience, all while staying within a single ecosystem.

Coming on June 24 at 12 PM local time on Flipkart is the Infinix Note 60 Pro 5G Pininfarina Limited Edition, in that Torino Black color we've also seen on the Note 60 Ultra. articles.title: Ultrahuman launches M2 Live metabolic health platform for continuous glucose monitoring
articles.description: Ultrahuman is best-known for its smart rings, but the company recently branched out with a red light therapy device. Today, the company is introducing the Ultrahuman M2 Live, a platform for continuous glucose monitoring. This is actually based on the Lingo Glucose Biosensor by Abbott. It’s available over-the-counter and does not require a prescription. M2 can also work with Abbott’s Libre 3 Plus sensor. The Lingo sensor streams data to your phone over Bluetooth LE, though you will need NFC to activate it. Continuous glucose monitoring • Metabolic Score It’s important to note...

articles.title: Here's why Apple is already dropping support for so many watches in watchOS 27
articles.description: At its Worldwide Developers Conference earlier this month, Apple unveiled watchOS 27. This will be rolling out in the fall. Perhaps surprisingly, watchOS 27 will drop support for a lot of older Apple Watches, including the original Apple Watch Ultra, released in 2022. That device was the first Apple Watch to cost a whopping $799, so many owners may still be using it. But come September, they will only receive essential security updates, as the watch is not getting watchOS 27. Asked why, Apple Watch and Health Product Marketing Manager Cait Dooley told TechRadar: With every software...

articles.title: Inside Tom Brady’s car collection: From a $200 Dodge Dart to a $3 million Bugatti
articles.description: Tom Brady's impressive car collection, estimated at over $5 million, showcases a remarkable journey from a $200 Dodge Dart to hypercars like the Bugatti Veyron. The assortment includes luxury vehicles such as Aston Martins and a Rolls-Royce, alongside his first major splurge, a Cadillac Escalade. This diverse garage reflects his immense success and evolving tastes throughout his NFL career.

articles.title: Census 2027: The pressures of counting India
articles.description: Census enumerators face several challenges in field, including heat, connectivity issues, and safety concerns; despite being a digital system designed for real-time monitoring, field workers say conditions on ground and supervisory instructions affect data collection.

articles.title: Amazon, Walmart, Cisco, Uber and Meta who raced to give AI tools access to employees are now seemingly leraning the 'hard truth' that AI is not ...
articles.description: Major tech firms like Amazon and Meta are curbing employee AI tool usage due to escalating costs. The shift from simple chatbots to powerful AI agents, which consume more computing power and are billed per token, has led to unexpected expenses. Companies are now implementing usage caps and encouraging more strategic AI deployment to manage these rising bills.
